title: Qwen2FlashAttention sliding windows are applied to wrong layers
issue_summary: Qwen2 used the wrong layer-selection condition for sliding-window attention, so the implementation applied SWA to the top layers instead of the bottom layers described by the config.

root_cause.summary: The condition was effectively reversed: layer_idx >= max_window_layers was used where layer_idx < max_window_layers was expected.
root_cause.basis: issue report and linked fix PR

symptom: The wrong subset of layers uses sliding-window attention, changing the computation graph even when max_window_layers is set to cover the lower stack.

local_reproduction.mapping: The local case runs the same toy stack twice, once with the reversed layer predicate and once with the corrected predicate, and records the resulting layer schedule and output delta.

issue_contract.trigger: Configure only the lower stack to use sliding-window attention and run a multi-layer decoder.
issue_contract.mechanism: The buggy predicate routes sliding-window attention to the wrong layer indices.
issue_contract.observable_symptom: The wrong subset of layers uses sliding-window attention and outputs change.

reproduction_scope.mechanism_level_claim: This case reproduces the reversed layer-selection predicate for sliding-window attention.
reproduction_scope.issue_level_observable_claim: This case reproduces the reported symptom that the wrong layers use sliding-window attention and outputs change as a result.
